sanskrit: सुखदुःखे समे कृत्वा लाभालाभौ जयाजयौ। ततो युद्धाय युज्यस्व नैवं पापमवाप्स्यसि॥
transliteration: sukha-duḥkhe same kṛtvā lābhālābhau jayājayau / tato yuddhāya yujyasva naivaṃ pāpam avāpsyasi
literal_meaning: Having made pleasure-pain the same, loss-gain, victory-defeat, then engage in battle; thus you will not incur sin.
contextual_meaning: Perform the act because it is intrinsically right, not because of its hedonic outcome; detach from the emotional valence of results to avoid moral injury.
psychological_state: Pre-action paralysis rooted in outcome-anticipation; needs shift to values-based engagement to prevent moral residue.
trigger_scenario: A surgeon hesitating to operate because the survival odds are 50/50; advised to focus on surgical excellence, not the scoreboard.
anchor_text: Do what is right, untouched by pleasure or pain, gain or loss, victory or defeat.
